final_prompt: An eye-level photograph of a serene indoor corner viewed from slightly left of center, facing a large multi-pane window on the RIGHT wall. The wall is painted a soft light blue with a matte finish, appearing darker under low light conditions. The window has a white frame divided into six rectangular panes, through which a dark night sky dotted with faint stars is visible. A shallow white windowsill runs the full width of the window. On the left side of the windowsill sits a small square ceramic pot in off-white, precisely 15cm tall, containing a single green plant with broad oval leaves that have pointed tips; the plant is softly silhouetted against the dark window. Sheer white curtains hang from a rod above the window, one panel on the right side drawn slightly open, partially visible on the far right. The floor is covered in a smooth, light blue surface with a subtle sheen, reflecting the cool moonlight. In the foreground, slightly left of center on the floor, lies a single small dried brown leaf. The room is illuminated by soft, cool moonlight filtering through the window, creating pale blue shadows of the window frame across the floor and lower wall. Photorealistic, sharp focus, natural colors. Perspective: Camera viewpoint: eye-level view facing the corner of a room, with the window on the RIGHT side of the frame; Surface materials and textures: matte finish on the walls and floor; glossy glass on the window panes; ceramic texture on the plant pot; smooth fabric for the sheer white curtains partially visible on the far right. Style reference: Spatial positions: the white square plant pot is on the windowsill at CENTER-RIGHT; the green plant with broad oval leaves grows upward from it. A single small dried brown leaf lies on the light blue floor in the FOREGROUND, slightly left of center. The wall is light blue matte on the LEFT, and the floor is a smooth, light blue surface extending across the lower half of the frame; Surface materials and textures: matte finish on the walls and floor; glossy glass on the window panes; ceramic texture on the plant pot; smooth fabric for the sheer white curtains partially visible on the far right.

constraints: The photograph is taken from an eye-level perspective, facing the corner of a room.
The large multi-pane window is located on the right wall of the frame.
The wall is painted a soft light blue with a matte finish.
The window has a white frame divided into six rectangular panes.
Through the window, a dark night sky with faint stars is visible.
A shallow white windowsill runs the full width of the window.
On the left side of the windowsill, there is a small square ceramic pot in off-white.
The ceramic pot is approximately 15cm tall and contains a single green plant.
The plant has broad oval leaves with pointed tips and is softly silhouetted against the dark window.
Sheer white curtains hang from a rod above the window.
One panel of the sheer white curtains on the right side is drawn slightly open.
The floor is covered in a smooth, light blue surface with a subtle sheen.
The floor reflects cool moonlight.
In the foreground, slightly left of center on the floor, lies a single small dried brown leaf.
The room is illuminated by soft, cool moonlight.
Pale blue shadows of the window frame are visible across the floor and lower wall.
The image is photorealistic with sharp focus and natural colors.
The camera viewpoint is an eye-level view facing the corner of the room.
The wall on the left side of the frame is light blue matte.
The floor is a smooth, light blue surface extending across the lower half of the frame.
The window panes have a glossy glass texture.
The ceramic pot has a textured appearance.
The sheer white curtains have a smooth fabric texture.
The white square plant pot is positioned at the center-right of the windowsill.
The green plant grows upward from the center-right of the windowsill.
The single small dried brown leaf lies on the light blue floor in the foreground, slightly left of center.
